overview: This image features a juvenile caiman resting on a giant Victoria amazonica water lily pad. The lily pad is notable for its massive size, which can reach up to ten feet in diameter. These caimans are typically found in the wetlands of South America, such as the Amazon or the Pantanal. The lily pads possess prickles on their undersides for protection, while the topside provides a sturdy resting spot for the reptile.
